description: Port 8 tools from gpt-minions TypeScript multi-agent system to Python CLI packages in this monorepo

userStories.title: Add shared HTTP client helper to cli_common
userStories.description: Multiple tools (web_search, web_crawl, sonar, image_gen, cron) need to make HTTP API calls with common patterns: reading API keys from env vars, timeout handling, and converting HTTP errors to structured ToolExceptions. Add a thin httpx-based helper to cli_common that the API-calling tools can reuse.
acceptanceCriteria: File `packages/cli_common/src/cli_common/http.py` exists with a function `api_request(method, url, ...)` that wraps httpx and raises ToolException on failure
Function accepts `api_key_env` parameter and raises ToolException with code MISSING_CREDENTIALS if the env var is not set
Function handles httpx.TimeoutException and raises ToolException with code TIMEOUT
Function handles httpx.HTTPStatusError and raises ToolException with code HTTP_ERROR including status code in details
`httpx` is added to cli_common's dependencies in `packages/cli_common/pyproject.toml`
Tests exist in `packages/cli_common/tests/test_http.py` covering: missing env var, timeout, HTTP error, and success path (all mocked, no real network calls)
`uv run pytest packages/cli_common/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/cli_common/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/cli_common/src/` exits 0
userStories.notes: Keep it minimal — just the shared HTTP patterns. Don't over-abstract.

userStories.title: Create web_search_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool that performs Google searches via the Serper API. The tool takes a query string and optional result count, calls the Serper API, and returns structured search results as JSON. Mirrors the gpt-minions web-search.ts tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/web_search_tool/` with standard layout: `src/web_search_tool/{__init__,cli,models,service,errors}.py`, `tests/`, `pyproject.toml`
pyproject.toml declares dependency on cli-common (workspace), httpx, pydantic, typer and defines entrypoint `web-search-tool = "web_search_tool.cli:app"`
CLI command `search` accepts `--query` (required), `--num-results` (default 5, max 10) arguments
Service calls Serper API at `https://google.serper.dev/search` using SERPER_API_KEY env var
On success, emits ToolResponse JSON with result containing list of search results (title, url, snippet)
On missing API key, emits structured error with code MISSING_CREDENTIALS
Tests in `packages/web_search_tool/tests/` cover: success with mocked API response, missing API key error, invalid num_results validation
At least one test marked `@pytest.mark.external` that hits the real Serper API
`uv run pytest packages/web_search_tool/tests/ -q` passes (without --all)
`uv run ruff check packages/web_search_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/web_search_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml dev dependencies and uv.sources
userStories.notes: No caching needed in initial version. Keep it simple.

userStories.title: Create web_crawl_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool that fetches and extracts readable content from web pages. Uses a crawl4ai service endpoint (CRAWL4AI_BASE_URL) to render JavaScript-heavy pages and extract markdown content. Falls back to direct httpx fetch + readability-lxml for simpler pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/web_crawl_tool/` with standard layout: `src/web_crawl_tool/{__init__,cli,models,service,errors}.py`, `tests/`, `pyproject.toml`
pyproject.toml declares dependencies on cli-common, httpx, pydantic, typer, and readability-lxml
CLI command `crawl` accepts `--url` (required), `--timeout` (default 60), `--max-length` (default 20000) arguments
Service attempts crawl4ai service first (if CRAWL4AI_BASE_URL is set), falls back to direct fetch + readability extraction
Output is truncated to --max-length characters with a truncation indicator
On success, emits ToolResponse JSON with result containing extracted text/markdown, url, and content length
Tests cover: successful crawl4ai response (mocked), fallback to direct fetch (mocked), timeout handling, URL validation
`uv run pytest packages/web_crawl_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/web_crawl_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/web_crawl_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: readability-lxml provides the Mozilla Readability algorithm in Python for extracting article content from HTML.

userStories.title: Create bash_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool that executes shell commands in a subprocess with configurable timeout and output limits. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code as structured JSON. This is the simplest tool — uses only stdlib subprocess.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/bash_tool/` with standard layout
pyproject.toml declares dependencies on cli-common, pydantic, typer only (no external deps beyond stdlib for execution)
CLI command `run` accepts `--command` (required), `--timeout` (default 30, seconds), `--max-output` (default 10000, chars) arguments
Service executes command via subprocess.run with shell=True, captures stdout and stderr, enforces timeout
Output is truncated to --max-output chars with indicator showing total length
On success, emits ToolResponse with result containing stdout, stderr, exit_code, truncated (bool)
On timeout, emits structured error with code TIMEOUT and includes partial output if available
Tests cover: successful command, command failure (non-zero exit), timeout, output truncation, empty command rejection
`uv run pytest packages/bash_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/bash_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/bash_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: Use subprocess.run with timeout parameter. Do not use asyncio for this simple case.

userStories.title: Create memory_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a file-based memory system with search, read, and write subcommands. Agents use this to persist and retrieve information across sessions. Memories are stored as files in a configurable directory with metadata headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/memory_tool/` with standard layout
CLI has three subcommands: `search --query`, `read --path`, `write --path --content [--append]`
Write validates that path is within the memory directory (no directory traversal via ../ or absolute paths)
Write creates parent directories as needed and supports --append mode
Read returns file content as structured JSON, errors with FILE_NOT_FOUND for missing files
Search scans all .md files in the memory directory and returns files whose content matches the query (simple substring match), sorted by modification time
Memory directory defaults to `./memory/` but is configurable via `--memory-dir` option on all subcommands
Tests use pytest tmp_path fixture for filesystem isolation — no writes to real filesystem
Tests cover: write + read roundtrip, append mode, search matching, directory traversal rejection, missing file read
`uv run pytest packages/memory_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/memory_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/memory_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: Keep search simple — substring matching is fine for v1. No embeddings or fancy relevance scoring.

userStories.title: Create browser_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a Playwright-based browser automation tool with subcommands for navigation, page inspection, interaction, and screenshots. This is the most complex tool — supports starting/stopping browser sessions, navigating to URLs, taking accessibility snapshots, clicking elements, typing text, and taking screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/browser_tool/` with standard layout
pyproject.toml declares dependencies on cli-common, playwright, pydantic, typer
CLI has subcommands: `start`, `stop`, `status`, `navigate --url`, `snapshot`, `screenshot --path`, `click --selector`, `type --selector --text`, `press --key`
`start` launches a headless Chromium browser and stores session state (PID + CDP endpoint) in a state file
`stop` closes the browser session and cleans up the state file
`status` reports whether a browser session is active
`navigate` loads a URL and waits for networkidle
`snapshot` returns the page's accessibility tree as structured text
`screenshot` saves a screenshot to the specified path and returns the path in the response
All subcommands that require a running browser emit BROWSER_NOT_RUNNING error if no session exists
Tests mock Playwright to avoid requiring browser installation in CI — at least 5 test cases covering start/stop lifecycle, navigate, snapshot, and error states
At least one test marked `@pytest.mark.external` that launches a real browser
`uv run pytest packages/browser_tool/tests/ -q` passes (without --all)
`uv run ruff check packages/browser_tool/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: Start with core actions (navigate, snapshot, click, type, press, screenshot). Additional actions like select, scroll, upload can be added later. Use CDP connection for session persistence across CLI invocations.

userStories.title: Create sonar_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool that queries the Perplexity Sonar API for AI-powered search with citations. Takes a query and optional model selection, returns a synthesized answer with source citations.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/sonar_tool/` with standard layout
CLI command `search` accepts `--query` (required), `--model` (default 'sonar', choices: sonar, sonar-pro, sonar-reasoning-pro, sonar-deep-research)
Service calls Perplexity API using PERPLEXITY_API_KEY env var
On success, emits ToolResponse with result containing answer text, citations list (url + title), and model used
On missing API key, emits MISSING_CREDENTIALS error
Tests cover: success with mocked response, missing API key, invalid model selection
`uv run pytest packages/sonar_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/sonar_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/sonar_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml

userStories.title: Create image_gen_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool that generates images using the Google Gemini API. Takes a text prompt and optional aspect ratio, generates an image, saves it to disk, and returns the file path.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/image_gen_tool/` with standard layout
CLI command `generate` accepts `--prompt` (required), `--output-path` (required), `--aspect-ratio` (default '1:1', choices: 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4)
Service calls Gemini API using GOOGLE_API_KEY env var
On success, saves image to output-path and emits ToolResponse with result containing path, prompt, aspect_ratio
On missing API key, emits MISSING_CREDENTIALS error
Tests cover: success with mocked API response (use a small test PNG bytes), missing API key, invalid output path
`uv run pytest packages/image_gen_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/image_gen_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/image_gen_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: Use the Gemini REST API directly via httpx rather than pulling in the google-generativeai SDK.

userStories.title: Create cron_tool package
userStories.description: Implement a CLI tool for scheduling jobs via an HTTP gateway API. Supports creating, listing, and deleting scheduled jobs with cron expressions, intervals, or one-shot timestamps.
acceptanceCriteria: Package exists at `packages/cron_tool/` with standard layout
CLI has subcommands: `create --name --schedule --command [--timezone]`, `list`, `delete --job-id`
Schedule parameter accepts cron expressions (e.g. '0 9 * * *'), intervals (e.g. 'every 5m'), and ISO timestamps for one-shot
Service calls gateway API at CRON_GATEWAY_URL env var
On success, create returns job_id and next_run_time; list returns array of jobs; delete confirms deletion
On missing gateway URL, emits MISSING_CREDENTIALS error
Tests cover: create job (mocked), list jobs (mocked), delete job (mocked), missing gateway URL, invalid schedule format
`uv run pytest packages/cron_tool/tests/ -q` passes
`uv run ruff check packages/cron_tool/` exits 0
`uv run mypy packages/cron_tool/src/` exits 0
Package is registered in root pyproject.toml
userStories.notes: This tool is a thin client for an external scheduling gateway, not a local cron daemon.
